title: 10 best herbal teas for gum pain
intro_desc: Herbal teas for gum pain are infusions made from plants and herbs that have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and soothing properties, which can help alleviate gum pain and promote oral health. Examples of herbal teas that can help with gum pain include chamomile, clove, calendula, and echinacea. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce gum inflammation and swelling, while clove tea contains eugenol, a natural anesthetic that can numb gum pain. Calendula tea has antiseptic and wound-healing properties that can help treat gum injuries and infections, and echinacea tea can boost the immune system and prevent gum diseases. By using herbal teas for gum pain, people can find relief from discomfort and improve their oral health, leading to a better quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tea helps with gum pain because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds that can reduce gum inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth. The tannins in sage tea also have astringent properties that can help tighten gum tissue, reduce bleeding, and promote healing. Moreover, sage tea has been found to have analgesic effects that can help alleviate gum pain and discomfort. Regular consumption of sage tea can help maintain good oral health and prevent gum diseases.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sage tea reduces gum pain by decreasing inflammation, thanks to the presence of rosmarinic acid and other phenolic compounds.
Antimicrobial: Sage tea inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria responsible for gum infections, owing to the antibacterial properties of camphor, camphene, and 1,8-cineole.
Antioxidant: Sage tea promotes gum health by neutralizing free radicals, which can damage gum tissue, due to its rich antioxidant content, including rosmarinic acid and various flavonoids.
Analgesic: Sage tea alleviates gum pain by acting as a natural painkiller, attributable to the presence of several volatile oils and tannins.
Astringent: Sage tea tightens and strengthens gum tissue, reducing gum pain and preventing gum damage, due to its astringent properties derived from tannins and essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried sage leaves to the boiling water.
Let the sage leaves steep in the hot water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove the sage leaves.
Sip the sage tea slowly, allowing it to reach the gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tea helps with gum pain because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth. The herb's active compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of periodontal pathogens, which can cause gum disease and pain. Drinking thyme tea or using it as a mouthwash can help soothe irritated gums, reduce redness, and promote oral health. Additionally, thyme tea's anti-oxidative properties can help protect gum tissue from further damage and promote healing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Thyme tea's anti-inflammatory property reduces gum pain and swelling, thanks to its active constituents, including flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Antibacterial: Thyme tea's antibacterial property helps combat gum pain by inhibiting the growth of harmful oral bacteria, due to its high thymol and carvacrol content.
Analgesic: Thyme tea's analgesic property alleviates gum pain, as its active constituents, such as thymol and carvacrol, interfere with pain signaling.
Antioxidant: Thyme tea's antioxidant property promotes gum health by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, thanks to its rich flavonoid and phenolic acid content.
Antiviral: Thyme tea's antiviral property helps protect gums from viral infections, contributing to overall gum health, due to its thymol and carvacrol content.
remedy_recipe: Measure - Boil 1 cup of water and add 1 teaspoon of dried thyme or 2 teaspoons of fresh thyme leaves.
Steep - Let the thyme leaves steep in the boiling water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain - Strain the thyme leaves from the water using a fine mesh strainer.
Sweeten - Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Serve - Drink the thyme tea while it is still warm, up to 3 times a day as needed for gum pain relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tea helps with gum pain because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the gums. The menthol in peppermint tea also has natural antibacterial and antiviral properties that can help kill harmful bacteria in the mouth, promoting oral health and reducing the risk of gum disease. Additionally, peppermint tea can help freshen breath and soothe irritation in the gums, providing relief from discomfort and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Analgesic property: Peppermint tea contains menthol, which acts as a natural painkiller and helps alleviate gum pain.
Anti-inflammatory property: The tea's menthol and rosmarinic acid content help reduce inflammation, providing relief for irritated gums.
Antibacterial property: Peppermint tea's menthol and other compounds, such as limonene and carvone, help fight bacteria that cause gum pain and infection.
Antioxidant property: Peppermint tea is rich in antioxidants, including rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, which promote gum tissue healing and help combat gum disease.
Antiseptic property: Peppermint tea's menthol and other active constituents have antiseptic properties, helping to prevent gum infections and further pain.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, place one teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves.
Pour boiling water (250ml) over the peppermint leaves.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the leaves.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to reach the gum pain area.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with gum pain because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the gums. The tea is also rich in flavonoids, which have antimicrobial and antioxidant properties that can help to prevent gum disease and promote oral health. Drinking chamomile tea can help to soothe and calm irritated gums, and its anti-inflammatory effects can help to reduce swelling and redness in the gums. Additionally, chamomile tea has a mild numbing effect that can provide temporary relief from gum pain. Regular use of chamomile tea can help to maintain healthy gums and prevent gum problems.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea can reduce gum pain by decreasing inflammation, thanks to the active constituents chamazulene and bisabolol.
Antibacterial: It helps to prevent gum infections, which can worsen gum pain, due to its antibacterial properties, attributed mainly to the chamazulene and alpha-bisabolol.

Antioxidant: German chamomile tea promotes gum tissue healing by fighting free radicals and reducing oxidative stress, thanks to its antioxidant properties, primarily from its flavonoids and terpenoids.
Analgesic: It alleviates gum pain by acting as a natural analgesic, with the active constituents Matricin and Bisabolol being responsible for its pain-relieving effects.

Antispasmodic: German chamomile tea can help to relax gum tissue and reduce muscle spasms, which can contribute to gum pain, due to its antispasmodic properties, primarily from Chamazulene and Bisabolol.
remedy_recipe: Measure - Boil 1 cup of water and let 2 grams of dried German chamomile flowers steep for 10 minutes.
Steep - After boiling, place chamomile flowers in a tea infuser and submerge in hot water.
Steep (cont.) - Allow chamomile flowers to steep for 10 minutes to release therapeutic compounds.
Strain - Remove tea infuser and discard used chamomile flowers.
Serve - Drink warm herbal tea up to three times a day for gum pain relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with gum pain because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth. The active compounds in licorice, such as glycyrrhizin, have been shown to help soothe gum pain and promote healthy gum tissue. Additionally, licorice tea can help to freshen breath and provide a natural alternative to traditional mouthwashes and treatments. It is important to note that while licorice tea can be beneficial for gum pain, it should not be used as a substitute for regular dental care and professional treatment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea's anti-inflammatory property, due to its active constituents like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, helps reduce gum pain caused by inflammation.
Antimicrobial: Licorice tea's antimicrobial property, from its glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria responsible for gum infections and pain.
Demulcent: Licorice tea's demulcent property, owed to its polysaccharides, forms a soothing film that protects and alleviates irritated gums.
Analgesic: Licorice tea's analgesic property, attributed to its glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, helps relieve gum pain and discomfort.
Wound healing: Licorice tea's wound healing property, due to its glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, promotes gum tissue regeneration and healing, reducing gum pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried licorice root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove the licorice root pieces.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to coat your gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with gum pain because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the gums. The tea is made from the flowers, leaves, and stems of the yarrow plant, which contain compounds such as flavonoids and alkaloids that have been shown to help with oral health. Yarrow tea can also help to stop bleeding and promote healing in the gums. Drinking yarrow tea or using it as a mouthwash can provide relief from gum pain and improve overall gum health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory property reduces gum pain by decreasing inflammation and swelling in the gums, thanks to active constituents like flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.

Antimicrobial: Yarrow tea's antimicrobial property helps alleviate gum pain by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria responsible for gum infections, attributable to active components like alpha-pinene and sabinene.
Astringent: Yarrow tea's astringent property assists in gum pain relief by tightening gum tissue and reducing bleeding, owing to tannins present in the tea.

Analgesic: Yarrow tea's analgesic property contributes to gum pain relief by numbing the affected area, with active constituents like chamazulene and azulene responsible for the pain-relieving effect.
Antioxidant: Yarrow tea's antioxidant property supports gum health by neutralizing free radicals and promoting tissue repair, primarily due to flavonoids and phenolic acids found in the tea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest two teaspoons of fresh yarrow leaves or one teaspoon of dried yarrow leaves.
Rinse the yarrow leaves under cold water to remove dirt or impurities.
Crush or chop the yarrow leaves to release their essential oils.
Steep the crushed yarrow leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the yarrow leaves before drinking the tea.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elderberry tea helps with gum pain because it contains anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can reduce inflammation and soothe irritated gums. The tea is also rich in vitamins and minerals that can promote oral health and help to strengthen gums. Additionally, elderberry tea has natural antimicrobial properties that can help to fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth that can cause gum pain and infection. Drinking elderberry tea regularly may help to prevent gum pain and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Elderberry tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce gum swelling and pain, thanks to the presence of anthocyanins and other flavonoids.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in elderberry tea, including anthocyanins and vitamin C, aids in tissue repair and healing of damaged gums.
Antibacterial: Elderberry tea's antibacterial properties, attributed to its polyphenols and other bioactive compounds, help fight oral infections causing gum pain.
Analgesic: Elderberry tea's natural analgesic effects, due to the presence of various flavonoids and anthocyanins, help alleviate gum pain and discomfort.
Immune-boosting: Elderberry tea's immune-boosting properties, primarily from its anthocyanins and vitamin C content, support overall oral health and help the body fight off gum-related infections.
remedy_recipe: In a saucepan, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried elderberries.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup, discarding the solid elderberry pieces.
Add honey or lemon juice to taste, if desired.
Sip the warm tea slowly, allowing it to soothe your gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tea helps with gum pain because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight infection in the gums. The tea is made from the leaves of the plantain herb, which contain tannins that can help to soothe irritated gum tissue. Additionally, plantain tea has been shown to have astringent properties, which can help to tighten gum tissue and reduce bleeding. Drinking plantain tea regularly may also help to promote overall oral health by reducing plaque buildup and improving gum strength.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tea helps reduce gum pain by decreasing inflammation, thanks to its active constituents, including flavonoids and essential oils.
Antibacterial: Plantain tea's antibacterial properties, due to the presence of tannins and other compounds, help combat gum pain caused by bacterial infections.
Astringent: Plantain tea's astringent effects, from tannins and polyphenols, aid in healing gum tissue and reducing pain.
Analgesic: Plantain tea's analgesic properties, attributed to its mucilage content, help alleviate gum pain and discomfort.
Antioxidant: Plantain tea's antioxidant properties, derived from its flavonoids and phenolic acids, support gum health by reducing oxidative stress and promoting tissue repair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest two large plantain leaves (Plantago major) from a healthy plant, rinse, and pat dry.
Tear leaves into small pieces, place in a pot, and add 2 cups of water.
Bring water to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the tea, discarding the leaves, and let it cool to a comfortable temperature.
Sip the tea slowly, swishing it around the mouth to cover gum areas before swallowing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with gum pain because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria in the mouth. The tea's active compounds, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, can help to soothe irritated gums and promote healing. Additionally, pot marigold tea has been shown to have astringent properties, which can help to tighten and strengthen gum tissue. Drinking pot marigold tea may also boost the immune system, helping the body to fight off any underlying infections that may be causing gum pain. Overall, pot marigold tea can provide natural relief for gum pain and promote oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea reduces gum pain by decreasing inflammation, thanks to active constituents like flavonoids and carotenoids.
Antimicrobial: It inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria that cause gum infections, containing active compounds such as essential oils and alkaloids.
Wound-healing: Pot marigold tea promotes gum tissue regeneration, thanks to its rich content of antioxidants and flavonoids that aid in tissue repair.
Analgesic: It alleviates gum pain by numbing the affected area, due to the presence of active constituents like salicylates and flavonoids.
Antiseptic: Pot marigold tea prevents the spread of infection in gums, as it contains powerful antiseptic agents such as volatile oils and tannins.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 grams of dried pot marigold flowers.
Boil 250 milliliters of water in a teapot.
Steep the pot marigold flowers in the boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture to remove the flowers.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to soothe gum pain.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tea helps with gum pain because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the gums. The herb is rich in rosmarinic acid, tannins, and allantoin, which promote healing and tissue regeneration in the gums. Drinking comfrey tea or rinsing the mouth with it can soothe irritated gums and provide relief from discomfort. Furthermore, comfrey tea's astringent properties can help tighten gum tissues, reducing the risk of gum disease and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey tea's anti-inflammatory property, due to its rosmarinic acid content, helps reduce gum pain by decreasing inflammation in the affected area.
Analgesic: Comfrey tea's analgesic effect, from its allantoin and rosmarinic acid, helps alleviate gum pain by numbing the area and reducing pain sensitivity.

Antimicrobial: Comfrey tea's antimicrobial property, attributed to its tannins and rosmarinic acid, aids in gum pain relief by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria responsible for gum infections.
Astringent: Comfrey tea's astringent property, due to its tannins, is essential for gum pain relief as it helps tighten and contract gum tissues, reducing pain and inflammation.
Wound healing: Comfrey tea's wound healing property, from its allantoin content, is crucial for gum pain as it promotes tissue regeneration and accelerates the healing of gum injuries.
remedy_recipe: Harvest or obtain 2-3 dried comfrey leaves, chopping them into small pieces.
Place the chopped leaves in a tea infuser or teapot, adding 8 ounces of boiling water.
Allow the tea to steep for 10-15 minutes, covered, for optimal extraction.
Strain the tea, discarding the leaves, and let it cool to a comfortable temperature.
Sip the cooled comfrey tea, gently swishing it around the mouth to soothe gum pain.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat gum pain with herbal teas is by using chamomile or clove tea. Chamomile has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce gum inflammation and pain. Steep a chamomile tea bag in hot water for 5-10 minutes, then let it cool down before applying the tea bag directly to the affected area. Clove tea contains eugenol, a natural anesthetic that helps alleviate gum pain. To use clove tea, steep cloves in boiling water for 10-15 minutes, then strain and rinse your mouth with the tea. Avoid swallowing the tea, as it can cause stomach upset. Remember to consult with a dental professional if your gum pain persists.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gum pain that are treated with herbal teas include mouth sores, inflamed gums, and throat infections. Herbal teas like chamomile and sage have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums. Licorice root tea has been shown to have antimicrobial effects that can help treat mouth sores and throat infections. Additionally, clove tea has numbing properties that can provide relief from mouth pain. As with any treatment, it's important to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new herbal remedies.
